2606.09549.title: SecureClaw: Clawing Back Control of LLM Agents
2606.09549.paper_summary: Tool-using large language model (LLM) agents face two distinct security failures: unauthorized external actions and exposure of sensitive plaintext inside the runtime before any final output check can intervene. Existing defenses usually protect one boundary, either the planner/runtime or the action sink, and therefore do not by themselves secure both surfaces. We present SecureClaw, a dual-boundary architecture that places authorization at the effect sink and plaintext confinement at the read boundary. Sensitive reads pass through a trusted gateway that replaces raw values with opaque handles and, in the evaluated deployment, bounded summaries as an explicit declassification interface. Writes that change external state follow a PREVIEW$\rightarrow$COMMIT protocol in which only a trusted executor may commit the exact canonical request authorized by policy. The runtime can still plan over summaries and symbolic references, but cannot directly dereference secrets or perform side effects. Across AgentDojo, AgentLeak, and Agent Security Bench (ASB), SecureClaw is the only defense we evaluate in a common harness that simultaneously retains usable task utility and achieves 0\% attack success rate (ASR) on ASB, 0.64\% ASR on AgentDojo, and 3.23\% overall leak on AgentLeak's attacked parity lane, which measures final-output and internal-relay leakage.
2606.09549.paper_summary_zh: 工具使用的大型語言模型（LLM）代理面臨兩種不同的安全失敗：未經授權的外部行動和在任何最終輸出檢查介入之前，敏感明文在運行時的暴露。現有的防禦通常只保護一個邊界，無論是規劃者/運行時還是行動接收端，因此無法單獨保護這兩個面。 我們提出了SecureClaw，一種雙邊界架構，將授權放置在效果接收端，並在讀取邊界上進行明文限制。敏感讀取通過一個受信任的網關，該網關用不透明的句柄替換原始值，並在評估的部署中，作為明確的去分類接口，使用有界摘要。改變外部狀態的寫入遵循PREVIEW$\rightarrow$COMMIT協議，其中只有受信任的執行者可以提交政策授權的確切標準請求。運行時仍然可以在摘要和符號引用上進行規劃，但不能直接解引用秘密或執行副作用。在AgentDojo、AgentLeak和Agent Security Bench (ASB)中，SecureClaw是我們在共同環境中評估的唯一防禦，該防禦同時保留可用的任務效用，並在ASB上達到0\%的攻擊成功率（ASR），在AgentDojo上達到0.64\%的ASR，以及在AgentLeak的攻擊對稱通道上達到3.23\%的整體洩漏，該通道測量最終輸出和內部中繼洩漏。
2606.09549.author: Yuhan Ma et.al.
